titleStmt.title: Letter from Max S. Green to Sir Matthew Nathan, 6 May 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Max Green

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Letter from Max S. Green (c.1865-1922), chairman of the Irish Prisons Board, to Sir Matthew Nathan. Nathan (1862-1939).Green writes that he is forwarding statements concerning the whereabouts of his staff between 22 April and 1 May 1916, the week of the Easter Rising.Sir Matthew Nathan was the under-secretary for Ireland from 1914 to 1916. In 1916 Nathan offered his resignation following the outbreak of the Easter Rising.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers, CSO RP/1916/7703/1

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
GENERAL PRISONS BOARD, DUBLIN CASTLE, 6th May 1916. Sir, With reference to your communication of the 1st
instant, I beg to forward herewith statements made by
members of this staff employed in Dublin of their
movements and occupations during each day, and of their
place of sojourn each night between noon on Saturday,
April 22nd and noon on Monday May 1st. I have the honour to be,
Sir,
Your obedient Servant, Max S. Green
CHAIRMAN. The Under Secretary, &c., &c., &c., Dublin Castle. Chief Commissioner
Esq 8/5/16
GENERAL PRISONS BOARD.
List of Members of Office Staff employed in Dublin.
(April 1916.) excluding Charwomen.
Mr. Max S. Green. Chairman
The MacDermot Vice Chairman.
(Dr. Edgar Flinn - Chief Inspr. of Reformatories & Member of Board.)
Mr S. H. Douglas Secretary.
.. Ed. Metcalfe - Controller of Industries.
.. J. J. Rafter - Clerk in Charge of Accounts.
.. J. White - Staff Officer.
.. T. J. Irwin - Second Division Clerks. .
.. T. H. Wright. (Higher Grade)
.. W. McClure - Minor Staff Officer.
.. H. P. Bell - Second Division Clerks.
.. T. F. Hurley
.. T. MacMahon
.. B. Abraham - Clerk of Works.
.. W. J. Hadden - Assistant and other Clerks.
.. W. Henry
.. R. Ryan
.. J. J. Young
.. P. Dalton Men Messengers.
.. G. Butler
.. G. Donaghey Boy Messengers.
.. F. McDonald
.. M. Bolger Office Keeper.
.. J. Bolton Agent for Discharged Prisoners.
